free_text: Photoneutron cross sections for Co, Ni, Cu and Ga
Natural CO, NI, CU, GA element targets, 2 g/cm**2.
The  PENFOLD-LEISS  method  for  cross  section
The results of measurements from the threshold to  28
MeV of total photoneutron cross sections for natural
cobalt, nickel,  copper and gallium are  given.
The  targets of the natural elements were exposed to
the g-ray beam of the 31 MeV betatron of the  Centro
Studi  Fisico-Biologici  of  Turin  University.  The
photoneutrons  were  detected  by   means   of   4PI
thermalized neutron  counter.  For the four elements
the integrated cross sections in mb*MeV  are:  Co  -
947 (98). For Co, Ni and Cu the (gamma,2n) reaction
cross sections were obtained using statistical theory
Data were compiled at the MSU SINP CDFE by

in SUBENTs  005 and 007, DATA in SUBENT 002.
Corrected  by SD and VV:  STATUS, DATA  at
The (gamma,2n) reaction cross section was  calculated
using  the  neutron  multiplicity  data  assuming  a
statistical decay of the GDR states.
Theoretical Nuclear Physics,  Woley, New York, 1952.
